CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-04-2021 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-05-2021 Continued To Jun-23-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Application For Leave To File Supplemental Amici Curiae Brief In Opposition To Plaintiffs' Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Feb-25-2021 Text Ruling

Application For Leave To File Brief Of Amici Curiae Impact Fund, California Rural Legal Assistance, Inc., And 16 Additional California Legal Services Organizations In Support Of Defendants Opposition To Plaintiffs Request For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Feb-25-2021 Text Ruling

Hearing On Order To Show Cause Re: Plaintiff'S Request For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Feb-25-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JASON S. MILLS, Esq. of Morgan, Lewis & Bockius LLP (Tel: 213-612-2500) for Plaintiffs National Retail Federation, National Federation of Independent Business, Relles Florist, Mayfield Equipment Company, and Abate-A-Weed, Inc.;
Attys. WILLIAM H. DOWNER, Esq. and VILMA PALMA-SOLANA, Esq. of the California Attorney General's Office (Tel: 916-210-6120) for Defendants California Department of Industrial Relations, Division of Occupational Safety & Health, Occupational Safety & Health Standards Board, and Douglas Parker, in his official capacity as Chief of the California Department of Industrial Relations.

Hearing Re: Plaintiff's Application For Preliminary Injunction.

The Court proposes allowing all Amici Curiae briefs to be filed, but will not allow the factual record to be expanded upon by Amici Curiae outside of Plaintiffs and Defendants' factual record, nor will the Court allow anything improper for the Court to take judicial notice of, nor will the Court allow any issues to be raised by Amici Curiae that have not been raised by Plaintiffs and Defendants.

The Court sets a deadline of 2/5/21 for Defendants' to file their responsive pleading to the Complaint.

Argument presented by counsel for both sides regarding Plaintiffs' Application for Prelimninary Injunction.

The Court sets a deadline of 2/5/21 for each side to file a supplemental brief no longer than 8 pages in length which may address the relief and severability issues raised by the parties at today's hearing or anything else the parties believe to be critical.

The Court will deem this matter submitted on 2/11/21.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
